_meta.description: Wave 4 production-quality governance policy for IQC/IPQC/OQC closure, QA/QC role split, and continuous-improvement reaction loops.

objective.name: Production And Quality Execution Closure
objective.statement: Every quality gate from incoming through in-process and outbound release must have one governed owner, explicit QA/QC role split, and a closed reaction loop into NCR/CAPA/SPC/MSA. Result rows and raw measurements remain subordinate evidence, never accidental lifecycle owners.

build_questions.question: This object exists to own exactly which gate: receiving, in-process, outbound release, inspection evidence, or reaction-loop escalation?
Which server-side roles may start, reinspect, disposition, waive, or close the quality gate, and where must QA authority stay distinct from production or logistics handoff?
Is this row a governed lifecycle owner, or only a result/measurement child that must never inherit the parent workflow?
Can the backend trace inspection plan, lot, execution result, and usage decision without split truth or alias drift?
If the process trends out of control or fails, does it escalate into NCR/CAPA and feed SPC/MSA or other continuous-improvement loops?
Is FQC a truly separate finished-goods gate, or only an alias of OQC until a distinct release authority and KPI exist?
Can an auditor trace from PO, SO, JO, WO, or shipment to inspection disposition, sample results, and follow-up action through one canonical chain?
If this quality element has no active operational purpose, has it been isolated into unused/archive instead of left live by habit?
